title: oligonucleotide_nanobot_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS F1 output rows
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/oligonucleotide_nanobot_cross.py. CROSS-AXIS integration F1: a DNA-origami staple strand IS an oligonucleotide, so the NANOBOT core-5 axis's (DNA-nanotechnology) origami assembly is bounded by oligo hybridization thermodynamics. For each staple in a deterministic origami staple panel, the OLIGONUCLEOTIDE expansion-main axis's SantaLucia (1998) unified nearest-neighbor model (oligonucleotide_hybridization_sim.py, imported verbatim — no fork) computes the staple:scaffold duplex dH/dS/dG/Tm, and fold-competence is gated on the NN Tm vs a folding-temperature gate. The SantaLucia 1 M Na+ NN Tm is illustrative, NOT a real Mg2+-buffered origami folding temperature; staple sequences are illustrative test inputs. In-silico simulator-consistency only (AGENTS.tape g8/f2) — NOT a structural / folding-yield / nanodevice-function / therapeutic / regulatory claim. No dG/dH/dS/Tm/count is derived from the n=6 lattice (g2/f_lattice_fit). A cross-axis bridge is NOT a new axis: the hexa-bio core-5 set (QUANTUM, WEAVE, NANOBOT, RIBOZYME, VIROCAPSID) is UNCHANGED.

staple_id.description: Identifier of the DNA-origami staple strand from the deterministic staple panel.

design_note.description: Illustrative design note describing the staple (length / composition class).

sequence_5to3.description: Staple oligonucleotide sequence, 5'->3', normalized to the DNA alphabet.

gc_fraction.description: G+C fraction of the staple sequence.

nanobot_axis_source.description: Path to the NANOBOT core axis source whose DNA-nanotech modality basis is read as structural text (actuation logic untouched — f3).

nanobot_axis_role.description: Role of the NANOBOT core-5 axis (DNA-nanotechnology actuation).

modality_basis_cited_by_axis.description: DNA-nanotech modality citations the NANOBOT axis declares for itself (Rothemund 2006 DNA origami / Seeman 1982 immobile junctions) — own precedent, never lattice-derived (g3/f1).

oligonucleotide_model.description: The OLIGONUCLEOTIDE-axis model string — must reference SantaLucia 1998 unified NN.

dG37_kcal_mol.description: SantaLucia NN duplex free energy dG at 37 C (kcal/mol). More negative = more stable staple:scaffold duplex.

Tm_celsius.description: van 't Hoff two-state melting temperature of the staple:scaffold duplex (C), from the SantaLucia NN model. Illustrative 1 M Na+ standard-state value, NOT a real Mg2+-buffered origami folding temperature.

total_strand_M.description: Total-strand concentration used for the van 't Hoff Tm (M).

folding_temp_gate_celsius.description: Folding-temperature gate (C): a staple is fold-competent iff its duplex Tm sits at or above this gate. Illustrative fixed value, NOT lattice-derived.

tm_margin_celsius.description: Tm_celsius minus folding_temp_gate_celsius (C). Non-negative iff the staple is fold-competent.

fold_competent.description: True iff the staple:scaffold duplex Tm is at or above the folding-temperature gate — the OLIGONUCLEOTIDE thermodynamic bound on the NANOBOT axis's origami assembly.

in_silico_caveat.description: Restatement that every row is in-silico simulator-consistency only (g8/f2).

illustrative_only.description: Always true — the SantaLucia 1 M Na+ NN Tm and the staple panel are illustrative; the row is not a prediction of real origami folding behaviour.
